title: Tyk Gateway - API Troubleshooting
description: Single-API deep dive: metrics → traces → logs. Progressive disclosure from symptom to root cause. Audience: Backend engineers, SRE on-call.

options.content:
## 🗺 How to use this dashboard

**Target audience**: Backend engineers, on-call SREs  
**Use when**: Investigating a specific API incident

**Step 1**: Set `api_id` variable (top of page) to the API under investigation  
**Step 2**: Set time range to the incident window  
**Step 3**: Follow the signal chain:

1. **Latency spike?** → Latency Attribution row → Is it Gateway overhead or Upstream slowness?
   - Gateway overhead → check middleware config, rate limiting, auth plugin
   - Upstream slowness → upstream service issue, not Tyk
2. **Error spike?** → Error Analysis row → What status codes? What response flags?
   - `AKI` = auth key invalid | `URS` = upstream 5xx | `BD` = bad destination | `RL` = rate limited
3. **Need the trace?** → Click a dot on the latency chart → Tempo trace opens
4. **Need the logs?** → Click "View gateway logs" on any error panel → Loki

panels.title: ℹ️ How to use this dashboard
P95 Gateway Latency
panels.description: Tyk's own processing overhead for this API.

panels.title: P95 Upstream Latency
panels.description: This API's backend service latency.

panels.title: Latency Breakdown: Total / Gateway / Upstream (P95 + P50)
panels.description: The most powerful troubleshooting panel. If gateway latency is flat and upstream spikes, the backend is the culprit — no log diving needed.

panels.title: Gateway vs Upstream Latency Share
panels.description: Visual attribution: what % of total latency is Tyk's processing vs backend? Shifts immediately when upstream degrades.

panels.title: Latency Percentile Table
panels.description: P50 / P95 / P99 × Total / Gateway / Upstream — full picture in one row.

panels.title: P95 Latency by HTTP Method
panels.description: POST latency 3× GET? Likely a request body processing issue.

links.title: 📋 View gateway logs for this API

panels.title: Response Flag Distribution
panels.description: Fastest path to error cause: URS = upstream 5xx, BD = bad destination, RL = rate limited, AUTH = auth failure, KEY = key not found.

panels.title: Error Rate by Path
panels.description: Which endpoint is failing — /checkout or /products?

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by(listen_path)(rate(tyk_requests_by_path_total{service_name="tyk-gateway", response_code=~"5..", api_id="$api_id"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum by(listen_path)(rate(tyk_requests_by_path_total{service_name="tyk-gateway", api_id="$api_id"}[$__rate_interval])) * 100)

panels.title: Request Rate Over Time
panels.description: Is the error rate spike correlated with a traffic surge?

panels.title: Request Rate by API Key
panels.description: Is one consumer driving the load?

panels.title: 429 Rate (Rate Limiting)
panels.description: Leading cause of 'API suddenly slow' support tickets.

panels.title: Upstream P95 Latency
panels.description: Backend service health over time for this API.

panels.title: Upstream Error Flags (URS)
panels.description: URS = upstream-originated 5xx, isolated from Tyk errors. When this rises, the backend is the culprit.

panels.title: Backend Version Distribution
panels.description: Did latency increase after a backend version change? Correlate with canary rollouts.

panels.title: APIs Loaded on Serving Nodes
panels.description: Did a node silently drop this API's config during a rolling deploy?

panels.title: Config Reload Events
panels.description: Correlate reload timing with when errors started.

panels.title: Config Reload Duration
panels.description: Slow reload (>30s) during high traffic can cause brief request failures.

panels.title: Distributed Tracing via Tempo (Signal Correlation Entry Point)
Recent Traces
panels.description: Recent traces for this API. Click Trace ID to open in Tempo. Use '+ Filter logs by this trace' to correlate with Row 8 logs.

panels.title: Error Traces
panels.description: Traces with error status for this API. Click Trace ID to investigate root cause in Tempo.

panels.title: Access Logs (via tyk-gateway)
panels.description: Envoy access logs for requests routed through tyk-gateway. Set $trace_id to a specific trace ID (copied from Row 7) to filter to a single request.

panels.title: Error Logs (tyk-gateway)
panels.description: Logs mentioning tyk-gateway as upstream. Filter for 4xx/5xx by searching the log line manually.

panels.title: All Recent Logs (trace correlation)
panels.description: Full log stream. Paste a trace_id from Row 7 into the $trace_id variable above — when set, this panel filters to log lines containing that ID, enabling cross-service correlation.

options.content:
[Error classification docs →](https://tyk.io/docs/api-management/logs#error-classification)

**Upstream and Proxy Errors**

| Flag | Name | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|------|-------------|
| `TLE` | TLS Certificate Expired | 502 | Upstream TLS certificate has expired |
| `TLI` | TLS Certificate Invalid | 502 | Upstream TLS certificate failed validation |
| `TLM` | TLS Hostname Mismatch | 502 | Certificate CN/SAN does not match hostname |
| `TLN` | TLS Not Trusted | 502 | Certificate issued by unknown authority |
| `TLH` | TLS Handshake Failure | 502 | TLS handshake failed |
| `TLP` | TLS Protocol Error | 502 | TLS protocol or version mismatch |
| `TLA` | TLS Alert | 502 | TLS alert received from upstream |
| `UCF` | Connection Refused | 502 | Upstream refused the TCP connection |
| `UCT` | Connection Timeout | 502 | TCP connection to upstream timed out |
| `URR` | Connection Reset | 502 | Connection reset by upstream server |
| `EPI` | Broken Pipe | 502 | Connection closed unexpectedly (EPIPE) |
| `URT` | Response Timeout | 504 | Upstream did not respond in time |
| `DNS` | DNS Resolution Failure | 502 | Upstream hostname could not be resolved |
| `NRH` | No Route to Host | 502 | Upstream host or network unreachable |
| `NHU` | No Healthy Upstreams | 503 | All targets in load balancer are unhealthy |
| `CBO` | Circuit Breaker Open | 503 | Circuit breaker is open |
| `URS` | Upstream Response 5XX | 5XX | Upstream returned a 5XX status code |
| `UPE` | Upstream Error | 5XX | Generic upstream error |

**Authentication Errors**

| Flag | Name | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|------|-------------|
| `AMF` | Auth Field Missing | 400/401 | Authorization header or parameter missing |
| `AKI` | API Key Invalid | 403 | API key not found or invalid |
| `TKE` | Token Expired | 403 | JWT, OAuth token, or client cert has expired |
| `TKI` | Token Invalid | 403 | JWT or OAuth token malformed or bad signature |
| `TCV` | Token Claims Invalid | 401 | JWT claims validation failed |
| `EAD` | External Auth Denied | 403 | External auth service denied the request |

**Rate Limiting**

| Flag | Name | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|------|-------------|
| `RLT` | Rate Limited | 429 | Request rejected due to rate limit |
| `QEX` | Quota Exceeded | 403 | Client usage quota exhausted |

**Request Validation**

| Flag | Name | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|------|-------------|
| `BTL` | Body Too Large | 400 | Request body exceeds configured size limit |
| `CLM` | Content-Length Missing | 411 | Required Content-Length header missing |
| `BIV` | Body Invalid | 400/422 | Request body malformed or fails schema validation |
| `IHD` | Invalid Header | 400 | Required request header malformed or invalid |

**Client Connection**

| Flag | Name | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|------|-------------|
| `CDC` | Client Disconnected | 499 | Client closed connection before response sent |

panels.title: Error Flag Rate Over Time (4xx+5xx)
panels.description: Rate of 4xx+5xx errors broken down by Tyk response flag — shows the WHY behind each error class. Uses http.server.request.duration histogram count.

panels.title: Error Flag × Status Code Breakdown
panels.description: Cross-breakdown of error flags, status codes, and APIs over the selected time range. Uses http.server.request.duration histogram count.

targets.expr: sort_desc(sum by(tyk_response_flag, http_response_status_code, tyk_api_id) (increase(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{service_name="tyk-gateway", tyk_api_id="$api_id", tyk_gw_id=~"$tyk_gw_id", http_response_status_code=~"4..|5.."}[$__range])))
